Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(9 inline comments)

Overall it looks good for me, but please use TABs for indention and fix check for array bounds.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(7 inline comments)

Its a good start to support clang. This will be especially important in the future for linux as well. I still like to keep the naming (ALIGN and __fls) as close to the linux client code that is upstream as possible.

Patch Set 11:

Something is wrong with the test. I looked at the logs and all the test reported as passed but it reports them as failed. Can someone look into this.

Patch Set 11:

Strongly suspect the tests really are failing, just not reporting properly due to LU-4344.  for instance from log last conf-sanity subtest run was 32a, but that isn't close to the end of conf-sanity.  think there are real test failures here.

Patch Set 11:

note the additional instances of node-provisioning and lustre-initialization in the test results after sanity and conf-sanity.  suspect subtests of killing one or more nodes causing early exits of test scripts and reloading of test nodes.  If so the reports of FAIL are very probably correct.
Uploaded patch set 12.

Patch Set 22: Code-Review-1

(8 comments)

It seems that this latest patch has lost a number of fixes that were made by James in patch #20, yet still didn't make fixes that were mentioned repeatedly in previous patches (see patches #17 through #21 for comments).

I'm ok with patches not fixing every style issue if they are otherwise ready to go, but if a patch is refreshed it should fix the issues reported in the previous versions, or provide some explanation why the proposed change is incorrect or unnecessary. Otherwise, the time taken for inspection is just a huge waste of time and the code will never get better.

Patch Set 28:

> Yu Jian, I only added you for the conf-sanity test clanup so you can take a look at that.

The change in conf-sanity test 36 is fine. Currently, the test is always skipped in autotest runs because it requires single test node. I think it's better to improve it to run with remote servers.

Patch Set 29:

(2 comments)

Shadow, please submit a patch IMMEDIATELY that fixes up the style issues and problems with this patch.

There were several minor problems that were reported multiple times on earlier versions of the patch that could have easily been fixed when this patch was refreshed, and were even fixed up by others that refreshed this patch for you, but the fixes were reverted by later versions of this patch.

Otherwise, I'm going to revert this patch to get rid of the completely useless addition of lvfs_callback_ops and l_fid2dentry.

There are several other issues with the version of this patch that landed.  They were mentioned repeatedly in previous reviews, and even fixed in the refreshed patches submitted by James Simmons.  You can go back and find them in those patches.

commit.message:
LU-3963 cleanup: C89 and build cleanups

use C99 initialization for a types, remove some platform depended code
and use POSIX compatible instead.
don't build a lhsmtool is none utils allowed.
